headlines.title: Mitsui Eyes Global LNG Deals to Power the AI Data Center Boom
headlines.summary: Japanese commodities major Mitsui is looking for investment opportunities in liquefied natural gas to secure the long-term supply of electricity for the booming data center industry. headlines.summary: Oil prices have pulled back sharply from recent highs, suggesting that the market was optimistically pricing in at least a partial reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and an eventual normalization, following positive statements from the US. headlines.title: U.S. Turns Cold War Plutonium Into Nuclear Fuel
headlines.summary: The federal U.S. government has turned to plutonium from the Cold War era as an alternative to uranium in nuclear power generation, as supply of the default nuclear fuel runs short of demand. headlines.title: Summer Heatwaves May Deepen the Global LNG Crunch
headlines.summary: The global gas market, which has been reeling from the sudden loss of 20% of daily LNG supply, is poised to tighten even further in the coming months as higher-than-expected summer temperatures and the El Niño weather pattern are expected to raise gas demand in Asia. headlines.title: Bernstein Sets Long-Term Oil Price Target At $75 Per Barrel
headlines.summary: Wall Street analyst Bernstein Research has established $75 per barrel as a reasonable long-term oil price target for equity valuations, pointing to rising marginal costs and declining reserves. headlines.title: US Crude Oil, Gasoline Inventories Continue Freefall
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 3.3 million barrels during the week ending May 22, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Thursday.
